full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-7915-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-7915-1: Radare2 vulnerabilities

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
8 December 2025

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
Several security issues were fixed in radare2.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 25.10

 25.04

 24.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

18.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 radare2 - free and advanced command line hexadecimal editor
 

 

 
Details

 

 

It was discovered that Radare2 contained several memory leaks. An attacker
could possibly use these issues to cause a denial of service.

 

 

 

It was discovered that Radare2 contained several memory leaks. An attacker
could possibly use these issues to cause a denial of service.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 25.10

questing

 radare2 – 

5.9.8+dfsg-2ubuntu0.25.10.2

 

 25.04

plucky

 radare2 – 

5.9.8+dfsg-2ubuntu0.25.04.2

 

 24.04

LTS noble

 radare2 – 

5.5.0+dfsg-1.1ubuntu3+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 20.04

LTS focal

 radare2 – 

4.2.1+dfsg-2ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 18.04

LTS bionic

 radare2 – 

2.3.0+dfsg-2ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

Reduce your security exposure

 

 Ubuntu Pro provides ten-year security coverage to 25,000+ packages in Main and Universe repositories, and it is free for up to five machines.
 

 

 Get Ubuntu Pro
